checkedConditions: CommAssignNext accepts an optional request_nonce propagated from swarm operation_id for explicit retry idempotency.
When assign_next is called without target_session and with request_nonce, duplicate calls replay the original AssignTask response instead of selecting the next unassigned task.
assign_next behavior without operation_id remains unchanged so intentional repeated assign_next calls can still assign additional tasks.
Focused protocol/server/tool tests, cargo fmt/check, git diff --check, selfdev build/reload, governance, commit, and push pass.
evidence: crates/jcode-protocol/src/lib.rs adds optional request_nonce to CommAssignNext and protocol roundtrip coverage validates serialization.
src/tool/communicate.rs maps explicit assign_next operation_id to an op-prefixed request_nonce while run_plan/fill_slots and calls without operation_id continue sending None.
src/server/comm_control.rs wraps target_session=None assign_next in an outer mutation keyed by swarm_id and request_nonce, finishing only after resolved assign_task response capture.
src/server/comm_control_tests/assign_next_dependency.rs proves duplicate request_nonce replays the original task-one assignment and leaves task-two queued.
Focused verification task 50282844x5 and selfdev build task 629485ai5d passed; reload succeeded.
notes: The idempotency key is explicit-only: default repeated assign_next calls remain able to advance to additional tasks, and target_session-specific paths still rely on existing assign_task mutation behavior.
